# UC-14.7.7 · Multi-Site Litmus Edge Fleet Health

## Description

Aggregating gateway health by site highlights WAN issues, deployment drift, and capacity hotspots across plants without per-host manual checks.

## Value

Aggregating gateway health by site highlights WAN issues, deployment drift, and capacity hotspots across plants without per-host manual checks.

## Implementation

Require site_id in every Litmus health payload. Schedule a nightly report by site for executive visibility.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Litmus Edge (Splunk HEC connector).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot` `sourcetype="litmus:health"` fields `site_id`, `gateway_id`, `status`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Require site_id in every Litmus health payload. Schedule a nightly report by site for executive visibility.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="litmus:health"
| eval site=coalesce(site_id, plant_id, "unknown")
| eval is_ok=if(match(lower(status),"online|up|connected|running"), 1, 0)
| stats count as events, sum(is_ok) as ok_events, dc(gateway_id) as gateways by site
| eval health_pct=round(100*ok_events/events,1)
| table site, gateways, health_pct, ok_events, events
| sort health_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Multi-Site Litmus Edge Fleet Health** — Aggregating gateway health by site highlights WAN issues, deployment drift, and capacity hotspots across plants without per-host manual checks.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot` `sourcetype="litmus:health"` fields `site_id`, `gateway_id`, `status`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Litmus Edge (Splunk HEC connector). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: litmus:health. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="litmus:health". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **site** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **is_ok** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by site** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **health_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Pipeline stage (see **Multi-Site Litmus Edge Fleet Health**): table site, gateways, health_pct, ok_events, events
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Run the search over a window that includes a known test or production cycle, then compare with the Litmus Edge admin console for gateway health, tag paths, and pipeline status. Reconcile site and device ids.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (health % by site), Table (site ranking), Treemap (gateways × site).

## SPL

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="litmus:health"
| eval site=coalesce(site_id, plant_id, "unknown")
| eval is_ok=if(match(lower(status),"online|up|connected|running"), 1, 0)
| stats count as events, sum(is_ok) as ok_events, dc(gateway_id) as gateways by site
| eval health_pct=round(100*ok_events/events,1)
| table site, gateways, health_pct, ok_events, events
| sort health_pct
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (health % by site), Table (site ranking), Treemap (gateways × site).
